mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue forecasts upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-occurring growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month acceleration in the industrial production index indicates tightening capacity utilization, prompting expectations of supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression that depress short-term equity valuations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of acceleration episodes, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 82%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase surpassing 2.5% triggers inflation fears, forcing markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of threshold crossings, or the signal loses significance when CPI growth remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 24-month rolling window, or 60-day returns show no statistical edge during payroll expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and declining housing starts signals a shift in consumer spending toward durable goods, creating sector rotation pressures that generate negative broad market forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the industrial production index during periods of high capacity utilization signals imminent supply constraints, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of decline episodes, or the predictive power vanishes when industrial production remains above trend.
